summary: Frontend-only verification completed for Netlify Forms migration on public URL. All requested Netlify markup and submit-flow checks passed, including inline success message and no backend API submit usage.
action_items: Optional hardening: in submitForm, verify response.ok before showing success to avoid false-positive success on non-2xx Netlify response.
critical_code_review_comments: React form markup is Netlify-compatible: name='inquiry', method='POST', data-netlify='true', plus hidden form-name field.
Static hidden Netlify form is present in public/index.html with matching 4 field names (email, business_account_name, phone_numbers_text, package_type).
Form flow uses fetch('/') with URL-encoded payload and does not call axios/backend /api submit endpoints in tested flow.
Potential reliability gap: success UI is shown without checking HTTP status (response.ok).
test_credentials: No auth flow in scope. /app/memory/test_credentials.md exists and is effectively empty.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Validated on https://wa-spam-shield.preview.emergentagent.com via browser automation: form attributes, hidden form-name, 4-field payload, no /api submit traffic, and inline success message after submit.
rca of the issue: No blocking bug reproduced for requested migration scope. Minor RCA: submit handler treats all HTTP responses as success because response.ok is not checked; only network exceptions trigger error path.
